prompt: The method should remove duplicates but returns the original array unchanged. What is the bug?
choices: The method builds the set correctly but returns the original arr instead of converting the set back to an array
The for-each loop duplicates elements instead of removing them
Set does not preserve insertion order so duplicates remain
explanation: The set correctly collects unique elements in insertion order, but the method discards it by returning the original arr. Fix: convert the set to an array, e.g., return seen.stream().mapToInt(Integer::intValue).toArray();
